description: Length-matched V2 protein structure facts. Phrasing rules applied: active voice, no hedging, matched certainty and length between truth and distractors.

facts.context: Enzyme active sites for small molecule drug targeting:
facts.truth: conserved catalytic pockets bind small molecules tightly
distractors: unconserved surface patches bind small molecules weakly
distributed exterior regions attract small molecules
transmembrane segments sequester small molecule ligands

facts.context: Allosteric drug binding sites differ from active sites because they:
facts.truth: alter activity through conformational shifts far from active site
distractors: share the same substrate pocket located in the active site
attach ligands without producing any conformational changes
modify catalytic residues through direct covalent attachment

facts.context: Protein-protein interaction (PPI) interfaces are challenging drug targets because:
facts.truth: flat surfaces spanning 1500-3000 A2 lack deep binding pockets
distractors: buried hydrophobic cores spanning large areas block all ligands
rapid partner dissociation across wide surfaces ejects compounds
high sequence divergence across broad contact zones blocks drugs

facts.context: PPI hot spots are residues that contribute disproportionately to binding energy:
facts.truth: Trp, Tyr, Arg each contribute more than 2 kcal/mol
distractors: Gly, Ala, Pro each contribute more than 2 kcal/mol
all interface residues contribute equal binding energy
peripheral charged residues dominate total binding free energy

facts.truth: hinge region between N-lobe and C-lobe, mimicking adenine
distractors: substrate groove on C-lobe exterior, mimicking phosphate
activation loop near phosphorylation site, blocking access
allosteric cleft between both lobes, displacing regulatory

facts.context: The DFG motif in kinases determines inhibitor type. Type II inhibitors bind when DFG is:
facts.truth: DFG-out, which exposes a hydrophobic allosteric pocket
distractors: DFG-in, which compacts the activation loop tightly shut
DFG-flipped, which opens a polar solvent-exposed channel
DFG-extended, which widens the catalytic substrate groove

facts.context: GPCR orthosteric binding sites for small molecule drugs are located:
facts.truth: inside the transmembrane helical bundle with extracellular entry
distractors: on intracellular loop surfaces adjacent to G-protein contacts
deep within the lipid bilayer at the membrane midplane level
across the extracellular N-terminal domain's exposed surface

facts.context: Antibody binding specificity is determined by:
facts.truth: CDR loops in variable domains that shape antigen contacts
distractors: Fc region glycan patterns that direct target recognition
framework beta-sheets that encode epitope specificity
hinge region conformations that define antigen selection

facts.context: Protein thermal stability (Tm) affects drug development because:
facts.truth: higher Tm yields longer shelf life and less aggregation
distractors: lower Tm yields better formulation and more solubility
Tm only determines the lyophilization cycle parameters
moderate Tm yields strongest receptor binding affinities

facts.context: Therapeutic protein aggregation is problematic because aggregates:
facts.truth: trigger immunogenicity, reduce efficacy, cause adverse reactions
distractors: extend serum half-life, increase potency, boost tissue uptake
improve thermal stability, shield patches, enhance solubility
increase tissue penetration, raise bioavailability, aid dosing

facts.truth: stabilize tertiary structure and require oxidizing conditions
distractors: form in reducing cytoplasm without any cofactor assistance
destabilize fold topology by constraining flexible loop regions
need enzymatic catalysis independent of the redox environment

facts.context: N-linked glycosylation of therapeutic antibodies affects:
distractors: manufacturing yield only, via improved folding efficiency
antigen binding affinity, through CDR loop stabilization
renal clearance rate, through glomerular size exclusion
